Program_Description: The Nurse-Family Partnership (NFP) Program is an evidence-based model program that helps young women take better care of themselves and their babies.
Target_Population: High-risk, low-income pregnant youth/women living in poverty.
Application_Process: Families are followed and supported using an intensive home visiting schedule until the child reaches the age of 2 years old.

Health_Department_Name: Los Angeles County Department of Public Health
Program_Description: Parents as Teachers (PAT) is a home-visiting program that provides parents and caregivers with information and support to help them become their child’s first and most important teacher.
Target_Population: Parents and caregivers of children from birth to age 5.

Health_Department_Name: Los Angeles County Department of Public Health
Program_Description: Healthy Families America (HFA) is a nationally recognized, evidence-based home visiting program aimed at preventing child abuse and neglect by providing support to parents during the critical early years of their child's development.
Target_Population: Families with newborns up to 3 months.
Eligibility_Requirements: Families are eligible to receive HFA services beginning prenatally or within three months of birth.

Health_Department_Name: Los Angeles County Department of Public Health
Program_Description: Project HOPE is a pilot program to support families experiencing homelessness, providing essential support during pregnancy and the early stages of childbearing.
Target_Population: Unhoused or unstable housing families.
Eligibility_Requirements: Pregnant or parenting with children through age 4 and reside in LA county.
Application_Process: Complete the Confidential Referral Form and submit via email.
